source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane / Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Overboard Discharge of Drilling Fluid
actions_taken: Driller instructed Derrickman to suspend transfer operations
Crew conducted investigation into fluid discharge location
Derrickman inspected lower processing room and dump line
BSEE inspectors arrived on location on 4/2/19 to conduct investigation
All documentation collected including JSAs, procedures, photos, and statements
event.summary: At approximately 0055 hrs on 1 April 2019, LLOG Exploration Offshore LLC experienced a spill of 61 barrels of Synthetic Oil-Based Mud (SBM) on the Seadrill West Neptune Drillship during drilling operations at Mississippi Canyon Block 199. The spill resulted from a misalignment of trip tank valve positions. While the Derrickman was transferring mud between trip tanks per driller request, the master dump valve for the Trip Tanks was found to be locked in the open position, allowing 61 bbls of SBM to flow into the overboard discharge line into the Gulf of Mexico. The pit cleaning crew had possibly locked open the master dump valve during cleaning operations and never returned it to its normally closed and locked position. Total pollution was 37.8 bbls (61 bbls at 62% synthetic make-up).

threats.name: Master dump valve left in open position
Improper valve alignment during mud transfer
Failure to verify valve positions before transfer

consequences.name: Overboard discharge of synthetic oil-based mud
Environmental pollution of Gulf of Mexico waters

controls.name: Master dump valve for Trip Tanks
controls.barrier_role: Primary containment barrier to prevent overboard discharge
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier designed to isolate discharge line when closed and locked

supporting_text: the master dump valve for the Trip Tanks was in the locked and open position, allowing 61 bbls of SBM flow into the overboard discharge line
This valve is a barrier in preventing drilling fluids from discharging fluids into the GOM when in the closed position
The pit cleaning crew may have locked open the master dump valve and never returned it to its normally closed and locked position

controls.name: Mud pit transfer procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper valve alignment and fluid transfer
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement for crew to follow proper transfer sequence and valve verification

supporting_text: The crew did not follow proper procedures in transferring mud
The Derrickman is in charge of verifying proper valve alignment before all fluid transfers according to fluid transfer procedures
Not following the procedures for mud pit transfer operations
After discussion with the Captain of the ship, the transfer was not considered normal operations for transferring mud from pit to pit

controls.name: Derrickman valve verification responsibility
controls.barrier_role: Human-based control to verify valve positions before fluid transfer
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement assigning derrickman responsibility for valve alignment verification

supporting_text: The Derrickman is in charge of verifying proper valve alignment before all fluid transfers according to fluid transfer procedures
Not verifying the master dump valve for the trip tanks locked in the closed position

controls.name: Driller monitoring of fluid levels via digital panel
controls.barrier_role: Detection and intervention control to identify abnormal fluid transfer rates
controls.lod_basis: Real-time monitoring system enabling early detection of discharge anomalies

supporting_text: While monitoring the fluid levels via the digital Driller's panel, the Driller noticed Trip Tank #1's fluid level was dropping as Trip Tank #2's was filling
After roughly two minutes, the Driller observed Trip Tank #1 dropped substantially more volume than Trip Tank #2 was filling
The Driller immediately contacted the Derrickman and instructed him to suspend transfer operations

controls.name: Pit cleaning crew procedures and handover
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure equipment is returned to safe state after maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement for pit cleaning crew to restore equipment to normal operating configuration

supporting_text: The pit cleaning crew may have locked open the master dump valve for the Trip Tank during cleaning operations, and never put back into its normally closed and locked position

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
